title: FSx for ONTAP — Forensic Investigation
description: User/IP/file-centric investigation views over normalized audit and FPolicy events. See docs/en/dii-capability-map.md for the capability mapping this dashboard implements.

list.name: user
list.label: User
list.description: Windows/AD username to investigate, e.g. CORP\\jdoe. Left blank matches all users.

list.name: client_ip
list.label: Client IP
list.description: Source IP address to investigate. Left blank matches all IPs.

list.name: path
list.label: File path
list.description: File or directory path (regex) to investigate. Left blank matches all paths.

panels.title: User Activity — ${user}
panels.description: All audit + FPolicy events for the selected user, chronological. Equivalent to DII SWS's Forensic User Overview.

panels.title: IP-Centric Activity — ${client_ip}
panels.description: All activity originating from the selected source IP, across users. Use for lateral-movement or credential-compromise investigation. Equivalent to DII SWS's Forensic User Activity Data drill-down.

panels.title: File / Entity History — ${path}
panels.description: All activity on the selected file/path, across every user who touched it. Equivalent to DII SWS's Forensic Entities Page.

panels.title: Operation Breakdown (active filter)
panels.description: Count of operations grouped by type, for whichever filter (user/IP/path) is currently narrowed. Helps spot anomalous action mixes (e.g. a spike in delete operations).

targets.expr: sum by (operation) (count_over_time({source="fsxn"} | json | user=~"$user" | client_ip=~"$client_ip" | path=~"$path" [$__interval]))
